### Post by stream303 on 2008-11-23
Interesting!

With Ubuntu booting to a black screen, do you mean that you are trying the OpenSuse xorg.conf with Ubuntu and it boots to a black screen with it?  Or do you mean that your current Ubuntu install (which one?) boots to a black screen?  AND, like my install, does it boot to a black screen without any splash or dmesg boot messages, but eventually gets to the gui unless you add the nosplash kernel parameter at either the second-stage yaboot boot prompt or put it in yaboot.conf?

Too many questions, I know. :)

If you are trying to substitute the OpenSuse xorg.conf with your Ubuntu installation, one thing you can do is to see where Ubuntu barfs on it with:

```
less /var/log/Xorg.0.log
```

and scroll up and down through the log to see what warnings or errors are thrown up.

I'd also be very interested to see what kernel parameters OpenSuse may be passing in the "append=" lines of /etc/yaboot.conf !

Thanks for all your help with the emac stuff by the way!

---

### Post by crapple on 2008-11-24
I am using ubuntu xorg in ubuntu. I had to do a fresh install because I had to do something to my harddrive to get it to work right. I didn't reinstall suse because the install takes over 5 hours and I didn't feel like waiting.

---

### Post by stream303 on 2008-11-24
Thanks for posting this.  With Hardy+ running the newer X, many of the former xorg.conf sections are optional, such as the files, inputdevice, dri, modules, etc.  I'm not sure what version OpenSuse is running on their release.

One thing I have noticed in many xorg.conf files, is that they are missing out on a nice improvement to the video by specifying the proper AGP speed.  I wonder if there are a lot of ppc boxes out there just running the default of 1X AGP because the installer just doesn't know about it, or if the newer X detects it automatically?  I suspect not, but then again I'm not an expert.

---

I have tried many combinations of options in the xorg.conf in order to get the radeon driver to work. In intrepid there doesn't  seem to be any errors in the Xorg log file; even the login sound comes through, but still a blank screen.

If you have found any way to fix this (ie. to use the radeon driver) please let me know. I am keen to hear of your progress ... :)
